master_row.title: Trawinistic Connection
master_row.canonical_statement: The Trawinistic connection generalizes the Levi-Civita connection so that covariant differentiation remains meaningful in a manifold with a degenerate metric at the zero point. It agrees with ordinary geometry away from the anchor and acquires singular behavior only where the null sector requires it.

master_row.dictionary: The Trawinistic connection (^T) is the unique affine connection satisfying (i) metric compatibility away from (p), (^T g = 0); (ii) torsion (T(^T)=0) outside (p); (iii) (^T) reduces to the Levi‑Civita connection when ( g 0); and (iv) (^T) annihilates null vectors at (p).
master_row.encyclopedia: The Trawinistic connection preserves ordinary geometry away from $p$ while extending the differential structure into the TZP degeneracy. Interpretive role: This operator is the backbone for every later derivative-based object in the registry. Once it is fixed, Laplacians, wave operators, curvature tensors, and transport laws all inherit a common singularly aware derivative structure.
